titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan papers
McLellan (David Stanley and Ann Handforth) Papers
titlestmt.author: by Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday July 16, 2025 at 23:01

did.unittitle: David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Writings, correspondence, journals, photographic prints, slides, audiovisual material, born-digital material, and other papers created by or related to David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 12 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 13 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged in six series: I. Correspondence, 1942-2009. II. Professional papers, 1938-2000. III. Family papers, 1945-2020. IV. Photographs and slides, 1945-2000. V. Audiovisual materials, 1944. VI. Computer media, 2009.

bioghist.head: David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan
bioghist.p: David Stanley McLellan (1924-2010) (Yale 1948, MA 1950, PhD 1954) was a professor emeritus of political science at Miami University. He served in World War II, flying B29s in the Pacific during the final months of the war. McLellan married Ann Handforth in 1945 while he was in basic training. After graduating from Yale, he and Ann went to Geneva, Switzerland and Grenoble, France where McLellan earned a License en Sciences Politiques from the University of Geneva.

He worked as a contract employee for the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) to scout potential airfields for Allied Forces in the Alps. Upon return to the US in 1955, McLellan and his family moved to Riverside, California where he taught political science at the University of California, Riverside. He joined the political science department at Miami University in 1970. McLellan focused on international relations and foreign policy in his academic scholarship and authored biographies on Dean Acheson and Cyrus Vance.

Ann Handforth McLellan (1925-2021) was an artist, activist, and frequent letter writer to Amnesty International. She earned a Bachelor of Arts from University of California, Riverside. Handforth McLellan supported her husband's academic career by organizing long-term research and teaching stays in Berkeley, CA, Washington, D.C., France, England, and Italy while also taking care of their four children. She proofread, edited, and typed McLellan's manuscripts for publication.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan Papers contains correspondence, writings, personal papers, photographs, negatives, slides, and audiovisual material that document the career and family life of political scientist David McLellan and his spouse Ann Handforth McLellan. The correspondence documents the early years of the McLellans' marriage as well as their lives abroad including when David served in the US Army during World War II and when he worked for the United States Central Intelligence Agency in the French Alps. These papers also document the McLellans' family life and many travels as well as David McLellan's career as a professor of political science at University of California Riverside and Miami University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Stanley and Ann Handforth McLellan Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

subject.part: Foreign policy of the United States

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into two sub-series: I. Incoming, 1951-2009. II. Outgoing, 1942-1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal correspondence between David and Ann McLellan and their family members. Correspondents include Ann's parents F. Hunter and Jessie Handforth, as well the McLellan's four children: Hilary, Michele, Marjorie, and Eric.
David McLellan's professional correspondence is also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by sender.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence sent to David and Ann McLellan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabeticaly by recipient.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence sent from David and Ann McLellan.

did.unittitle: Handforth, Jane - Yale and World War II

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents David McLellan's career as a professor of political science. The papers include research notes, drafts, project proposals, course materials, and interviews.

did.unittitle: Group think and the war in Vietnam
I.R. [International Relations] theory and foreign policy Steven Smith Journal of International Studies summer 1987
Notes to Margie regarding teaching ideas

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains David and Ann McLellan's family life and includes memoirs, family stories, and travel writings.

did.unittitle: "Bordeaux and Other Journeys" by Eric, Hilary, Margie, and Michele McLellan
"The McLellan-Handforth Family History: A Personal Story" by Eric McLellan
Memoir by David McLellan with Ann McLellan
Poem about Prince Edward Island neighbors by David McLellan
Seawood Estates summer house, [Prince Edward Island] PEI

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains David and Ann McLellan's family life and travels through photographs and slides. Along with their children, Michele, Eric, Marjorie, and Hilary, David and Ann's travels included trips to France, Italy, Spain, Arizona, and Prince Edward Island.

did.unittitle: Washington D.C. and east coast slides
Cambridge, Lake Como, and academic travel slides
Bordeaux, Le Bouscat, and beach slides
Paris, Bordeaux, Paum and Pamplona slides
Ann and David McLellan Europe photographs
Portrait negatives of Michele, Eric, Marjorie, and Hilary as kids
Miami-Western and Prince Edward Island 35 mm negatives

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a recorded interview by David McLellan and an audio letter David sent to Ann McLellan during World War II.

did.unittitle: "A 'Letter on a Record'" / from David S. McLellan 1st Nav Trng Grp Honda AAF, Tex / to Miss Ann Handsforth Spring Valley Rd Ossining, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains memorial videos for Hilary McLellan, daughter of David and Ann McLellan.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Hilary artifacts, archetypes, and rememberances: a film by John Carlson for A Celebration of Hilary 10-24-09
